message:
fix: block expression handling and allocator param issues

- Fix any()/all()/sum() to handle block expressions properly
- Fix unused allocator parameter detection in unittest methods
- Fix for loop iteration over block expressions
- Add producesBlockExpression() helper to detect Zig block expressions
- Fix list literals not counting as using allocator param
- Update test files to avoid variable reuse type conflicts

Unit tests: 29/29 passing

files.patch:
@@ -1058,3 +1058,25 @@ This project includes patent grants for all compression algorithms and optimizat
 ## Contributing
 
 See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) (coming soon)
+
+
+
+The "Shadow Import"
+Use standard Python import syntax, but let your compiler be smarter than the standard Python interpreter.
+Do this instead:
+code
+Python
+# In train.py
+import metal0
+
+# OPTION A: The Implicit Way (Best for DX)
+# If 'kernels.zig' exists in the folder, metal0 prefers it over kernels.py
+import kernels 
+
+# OPTION B: The Explicit Way (Best for Clarity)
+# Valid Python syntax, but the compiler statically replaces this 
+# with the linked Zig binary at compile time.
+kernels = metal0.load("kernels.zig")
+
+
+to allow manual memory control
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -16,6 +16,7 @@ fn producesBlockExpression(expr: ast.Node) bool {
         .subscript => true, // lines[idx] generates blk: {...}
         .list => true, // [1,2,3] generates block expression
         .dict => true, // {k:v} generates block expression
+        .set => true, // {1,2,3} generates block expression
         .listcomp => true, // [x for x in y] generates block
         .dictcomp => true, // {k:v for...} generates block
         .genexp => true, // (x for x in y) generates block
